reasoning: Provide a concise summary of the article, highlighting who, what, where, when, and key details such as attire, officiant, photographer, and notable circumstances.
output: Bridget and Jonathan Reid from the Highlands were married on skis at Nevis Range near Fort William, choosing the top of Easy Gully for the ceremony officiated by Halde Pottinger of the Humanist Society of Scotland. The couple, whose first date six years ago was a skiing trip, wore full Highland dress—kilted attire and a white wedding dress—against a snow-covered mountain backdrop. Adventure photographer Hamish Frost captured the moments. They benefited from recent snowfall and clear blue skies, describing it as the best conditions of the year. Bridget is a teacher and Jonathan runs an electrical automation company. The couple thanked Nevis Range staff for making their day possible and for helping host a party in the evening, and they joked about possibly being the first ski-wedding ever officiated and vowed on skis.
